UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the AscendFolio application. This entity stores user-specific preferences and identifying details, but explicitly excludes authentication credentials as per security best practices.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and identification. Must be a valid email format.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

role.description: The role assigned to the user within the application (e.g., 'user', 'admin'). This determines permissions and access levels.

currencyPreference.description: The preferred currency for displaying financial values across the user's portfolios (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile information was updated.

Portfolio.title: Portfolio
Portfolio.description: Represents a user's personal collection or grouping of investment holdings, allowing for organization and separate tracking of assets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Portfolio entity.

name.description: A user-defined name for the portfolio (e.g., 'Retirement Fund', 'Kids' College Savings').

description.description: An optional detailed description of the portfolio's purpose, strategy, or specific goals.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that owns this portfolio. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Portfolio)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the portfolio was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the portfolio's details were updated.

InvestmentAsset.title: InvestmentAsset
InvestmentAsset.description: Represents a general type of investment instrument (e.g., a specific stock, ETF, or cryptocurrency). This entity serves as a global catalog of tradable assets that users can track or hold.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvestmentAsset entity.

symbol.description: The unique ticker symbol or short identifier for the asset (e.g., 'AAPL' for Apple Inc., 'BTC' for Bitcoin, 'SPY' for SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust).

name.description: The full, official name of the investment asset (e.g., 'Apple Inc.', 'Bitcoin', 'SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust').

type.description: The category or class of the investment asset (e.g., 'Stock', 'ETF', 'Cryptocurrency', 'Mutual Fund').

exchange.description: The primary exchange or platform where the asset is traded (e.g., 'NASDAQ', 'NYSE', 'Coinbase').

currency.description: The base currency in which the asset is primarily denominated or traded (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'USDT').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this investment asset definition was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time this investment asset definition was updated.

Holding.title: Holding
Holding.description: Represents a specific quantity of an InvestmentAsset held within a user's Portfolio. This is the record of a user's ownership of a particular asset.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Holding entity.

portfolioId.description: Reference to the Portfolio that contains this holding. (Relationship: Portfolio 1:N Holding)

investmentAssetId.description: Reference to the specific InvestmentAsset (e.g., Apple stock, Bitcoin) being held. (Relationship: InvestmentAsset 1:N Holding)

quantity.description: The number of units, shares, or coins of the investment asset currently held.

averageCostBasis.description: The average price paid per unit for the total quantity held, used to calculate capital gains or losses.

initialPurchaseDate.description: The date of the first recorded transaction that contributed to this specific holding.

notes.description: Optional notes or comments associated with this specific holding.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this holding record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time this holding record was updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. Access is restricted to the user themselves or administrators. The document `id` MUST match the `{userId}` from the path (i.e., `request.auth.uid`). This collection serves as the primary source of truth for user roles and preferences.

This auxiliary collection contains marker documents where the `id` of the document is the `userId` of an administrator. The *existence* of a document at `/roles_admin/{userId}` grants administrative privileges, allowing efficient and atomic role checks in security rules (DBAC). This collection is denormalized from the `UserProfile.role` field to enable 'existence over content' checks.

Contains portfolios belonging to a specific user. The `userProfileId` field within each document MUST match the `{userId}` from the path, ensuring direct ownership verification for authorization independence. This structure supports QAPs for listing user-specific portfolios efficiently.

Stores specific investment holdings within a user's portfolio. To achieve authorization independence and avoid `get()` calls in rules, the `Holding` document will be augmented to include a denormalized `userProfileId` (matching `{userId}` from the path) in addition to its `portfolioId`. This ensures ownership verification directly from the document itself, supporting QAPs for listing user-specific holdings within a portfolio.

A global catalog of all available investment assets. All authenticated users have read-only access to this collection. Only administrators have write access. This collection maintains a homogeneous security posture, enabling QAPs for listing all assets for all users.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for AscendFolio prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access control by strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved primarily through structural segregation and strategic denormalization. User-owned data (`UserProfile`, `Portfolio`, `Holding`) is organized hierarchically under `/users/{userId}`. For nested subcollections like `holdings`, critical ownership identifiers (`userProfileId`) are denormalized into each `Holding` document. For example, a `Holding` document at `/users/{userId}/portfolios/{portfolioId}/holdings/{holdingId}` will explicitly contain `userProfileId` matching `{userId}` and `portfolioId` matching `{portfolioId}`. This eliminates the need for expensive and non-atomic `get()` calls in security rules to verify parent ownership, making rules simple to write and highly performant. The global `InvestmentAsset` collection has no hierarchical dependencies, inherently supporting authorization independence for its specific use case.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure supports efficient and secure `list` operations by ensuring homogeneous security postures within each collection or subcollection, and by embedding all necessary authorization context directly within documents. 
1.  **Private User Data:** For `/users/{userId}/userProfile`, `/users/{userId}/portfolios/{portfolioId}`, and `/users/{userId}/portfolios/{portfolioId}/holdings/{holdingId}`, access is strictly tied to `request.auth.uid`. A user can only `list` or `get` their own data, as enforced by the `{userId}` wildcard matching `request.auth.uid`. This prevents unauthorized users from attempting to query data they don't own, adhering to the QAP principle.
2.  **Global Assets:** The `/investment_assets/{assetId}` collection is designed with a homogeneous security posture: all authenticated users have read-only access, while only administrators have write access. This allows users to `list` all available assets without additional filtering logic in rules, as every document is equally readable.
3.  **Admin Roles:** The `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection uses 'existence over content' for admin checks. Security rules can check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` to grant elevated privileges, enabling comprehensive control for administrators over all data, as per the user's request.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:** 
*   Private data (e.g., `UserProfile`) is stored under `/users/{userId}`. 
*   User-owned, one-to-many relationships (e.g., `User` has `Portfolios`, `Portfolio` has `Holdings`) follow the hierarchical path `/users/{userId}/portfolios/{portfolioId}` and `/users/{userId}/portfolios/{portfolioId}/holdings/{holdingId}`. 
*   Global, non-user-specific data (`InvestmentAsset`) resides in its own top-level collection (`/investment_assets/{assetId}`). 
*   Admin roles are managed via a dedicated 'marker' collection (`/roles_admin/{userId}`) for efficient DBAC.
